question.text: Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, within how many days must an intermediary redress an investor grievance?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, all SEBI-registered intermediaries are required to redress investor grievances within 45 days. This is the general timeline applicable to all intermediaries including brokers, depository participants, merchant bankers, and others.
**The Trap:** The 21-day timeline specifically applies to RTAs under SEBI RTA Regulations — a more stringent requirement. Candidates must distinguish between the general intermediary timeline (45 days) and the RTA-specific timeline (21 days). The exam frequently tests this distinction.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This 45-day timeline appears in NISM Series V-A (Mutual Funds), Series VIII (Equity Derivatives), and Series VI (Depository Operations). It is the default grievance redressal standard unless a specific regulation prescribes a shorter timeline for a particular intermediary type.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** While the regulation allows 45 days, best practice is to resolve complaints within 15-20 days. This builds investor trust and reduces the risk of escalation to SEBI SCORES. Maintain a three-tier escalation matrix: Level 1 (operations team, 7 days), Level 2 (compliance officer, 15 days), Level 3 (management, 30 days).
